title: C++ Vectors
content:
### Vectors

`std::vector` is a dynamic array that can grow and shrink at runtime. It is part of the C++ Standard Template Library (STL).

---

### Declaration and Initialization

```cpp
#include <vector>

vector<int> vec1;              // Empty vector
vector<int> vec2(10);          // 10 elements, default-initialized (0)
vector<int> vec3(5, 100);     // 5 elements, all 100
vector<int> vec4 = {1, 2, 3}; // Initializer list (C++11)
vector<int> vec5(vec4);       // Copy constructor
```

---

### Common Operations

| Operation | Code | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Add element | `vec.push_back(10)` | Append to end |
| Remove last | `vec.pop_back()` | Remove last element |
| Size | `vec.size()` | Number of elements |
| Capacity | `vec.capacity()` | Allocated storage |
| Access | `vec[0]` or `vec.at(0)` | Element at index |
| Insert | `vec.insert(pos, val)` | Insert at position |
| Erase | `vec.erase(pos)` | Remove at position |
| Clear | `vec.clear()` | Remove all elements |
| Empty | `vec.empty()` | Check if empty |

---

### Iterating

```cpp
vector<int> nums = {10, 20, 30};

// Index-based
for (size_t i = 0; i < nums.size(); i++) {
  cout << nums[i] << " ";
}

// Range-based
for (int n : nums) {
  cout << n << " ";
}

// Iterator
for (auto it = nums.begin(); it != nums.end(); ++it) {
  cout << *it << " ";
}
```

---

### Memory Management

| Property | std::vector | C-style array |
|----------|-------------|---------------|
| Dynamic size | Yes | No |
| Bounds checking | `.at()` throws | None |
| Memory managed | Automatic | Manual |
| STL algorithm compatible | Yes | Limited |

---

> **Key Point:** Prefer `std::vector` over C-style arrays in modern C++. It is safer, more flexible, and integrates well with STL algorithms.
